marketState: neutral-to-bearish - near-term heat offset by storage surplus and a soft seasonal skew
summary: Front price ($2.940) is sitting right at modeled fair value ($2.917 now, drifting to $3.036 by +21D) across every horizon, so there is no large mispricing edge either way. Seasonality leans lower: price is already -10.3pp below its typical seasonal path, the probability of being higher is only 31-50% over 1-4 weeks, and the typical 4-week move is -3.1% (though reliability is flagged Low, a 50pp spread). Storage is +184 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +72 Bcf build surprise, reinforcing a supply-heavy backdrop. Near-term weather is the one clearly bullish input - 1-7D demand is +21% vs 5y and above the historical max range - but the 8-16D outlook moderates back toward normal, and technicals show a bearish trend with a 50<200 EMA cross on every single timeframe from 5m to 1w even as daily/4h RSI (26-38) looks oversold enough for a bounce. FuturesCurve data is missing from this snapshot, which lowers confidence in the short-term structural read.

longTerm.rationale: Storage running +184 Bcf above the 5y average with a bearish surprise build, seasonality probHigher of only 31-50% and a -3.1% typical 4-week move, and managed money already net short (though only 35th percentile, leaving room to add) all point lower over a multi-week horizon; fair value is roughly flat/fair so there's no long-side mispricing to lean on.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained break and close above $3.25 (above the fair-value band ceiling for +14D/+21D), or a storage deficit surprise / extended heat wave that flips the 8-16D weather outlook materially above 5y normal.

shortTerm.rationale: Near-term weather (1-7D demand +21% vs 5y, above the historical max) is bullish, but every timeframe from 5m to 1w shows a bearish EMA cross and the 5m/15m frames already look stretched (above upper Bollinger, RSI 59-66), so the very short-term picture is genuinely conflicted. FuturesCurve data is missing from this snapshot, removing a key input for reading near-term term-structure.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a pullback into the $2.75-$2.80 fair-value/support zone to buy the heat-demand bounce, or a confirmed close below $2.75 with the 8-16D weather outlook staying near-normal to confirm downside continuation. Re-check once futuresCurve data is populated.
